subject: Chemistry
q:
For the given hypothetical reactions, the equilibrium constants are as follows :
\(\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} ; \mathrm{K}_1=1.0 \\
& \mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} ; \mathrm{K}_2=2.0 \\
& \mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W} ; \mathrm{K}_3=4.0
\end{aligned}\)
The equilibrium constant for the reaction \(\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W}\) is
question:
For the given hypothetical reactions, the equilibrium constants are as follows :
\(\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} ; \mathrm{K}_1=1.0 \\
& \mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} ; \mathrm{K}_2=2.0 \\
& \mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W} ; \mathrm{K}_3=4.0
\end{aligned}\)
The equilibrium constant for the reaction \(\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{W}\) is
solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}
\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Y} & \mathrm{k}_1=1 \\
\mathrm{Y} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Z} & \mathrm{k}_2=2 \\
\mathrm{Z} \rightleftharpoons \omega & \mathrm{k}_3=4 \\
\mathrm{X} \rightleftharpoons \omega & \mathrm{k}_1 \cdot \mathrm{k}_2 \cdot \mathrm{k}_3 \\
& \mathrm{k}=1 \times 2 \times 4 \\
& \mathrm{k}=8
\end{array}$

subject: Chemistry
q: In reaction $\mathrm{A}+2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}$, initial concentration of $\mathrm{B}$ was $1.5$ times of $\mathrm{[A]}$, but at equilibrium the concentrations of $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ became equal. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is :
question: In reaction $\mathrm{A}+2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}$, initial concentration of $\mathrm{B}$ was $1.5$ times of $\mathrm{[A]}$, but at equilibrium the concentrations of $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ became equal. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is :
solution: Hence $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{(2 x)^2 \times x}{(a-x)(1.5 a-2 x)^2}$ Given, at equilibrium $\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & (a-x)=(1.5 a-2 x) \\ \therefore & a=2 x\end{array}$ On solving $K_C=4$

The value of $\mathrm{Kc}$ for the reaction $\mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ at the same temperature is

subject: Chemistry
q: At a certain temperature in a 5L vessel, 2 moles of carbon monoxide and 3 moles of chlorine were allowed to reach equilibrium according to the reaction, $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{Cl}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{COCl}_2$. At equilibrium, if one mole of $\mathrm{CO}$ is present then equilibrium constant $\left(K_{\mathrm{c}}\right)$ for the reaction is:
question: At a certain temperature in a 5L vessel, 2 moles of carbon monoxide and 3 moles of chlorine were allowed to reach equilibrium according to the reaction, $\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{Cl}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{COCl}_2$. At equilibrium, if one mole of $\mathrm{CO}$ is present then equilibrium constant $\left(K_{\mathrm{c}}\right)$ for the reaction is:
solution:
Initially 2 moles of $\mathrm{CO}$ are present.
At equilibrium, 1 mole of $\mathrm{CO}$ is present Hence, $2-1=1$ moles of $\mathrm{CO}$ has reacted. 1 mole of CO will react with 1 mole of $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ to form 1 mole of $\mathrm{COCl}_2$. $3-1=2$ moles of $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ remains at equilibrium The equilibrium constant
$$
K_{\mathrm{c}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{COCl}_2\right]}{[\mathrm{CO}]\left[\mathrm{Cl}_2\right]}=\frac{\frac{1 \mathrm{~mol}}{5 \mathrm{~L}}}{\frac{1 \mathrm{~mol}}{5 \mathrm{~L}} \times \frac{2 \mathrm{~mol}}{5 \mathrm{~L}}}=2.5
$$

The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is <u>               </u> (Integer answer) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>08206</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>dm</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>atm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>

nT=1.93=1+2α

Kp=0.4651.93×190.931.93×1.920.5351.93×1.9

≃73×10-2 atm2

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the general reaction given below at 400 K
$x \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons y \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g})$
The values of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ are studied under the same condition of temperature but variation in $x$ and $y$.
(i) $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=85.87$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=2.586$ appropriate units
(ii) $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=0.862$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=28.62$ appropriate units.
The values of $x$ and $y$ in (i) and (ii) respectively are:
question:
Consider the general reaction given below at 400 K
$x \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons y \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g})$
The values of $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ are studied under the same condition of temperature but variation in $x$ and $y$.
(i) $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=85.87$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=2.586$ appropriate units
(ii) $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=0.862$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=28.62$ appropriate units.
The values of $x$ and $y$ in (i) and (ii) respectively are:
solution:
The relationship between $K_p$ and $K_c$ is given by $K_p = K_c(RT)^{\Delta n_g}$, where $\Delta n_g = y - x$.
Given $T = 400$ K and $R = 0.0821$ L atm K$^{-1}$ mol$^{-1}$.
The value of $RT = 0.0821 \times 400 = 32.84$.
For case (i): $K_p = 85.87$ and $K_c = 2.586$.
$\frac{K_p}{K_c} = \frac{85.87}{2.586} \approx 33.2$.
Since $33.2 \approx 32.84^1$, we have $\Delta n_g = y - x = 1$.
Checking options for (i):
(1) $y-x = 3-1 = 2$
(2) $y-x = 1-4 = -3$
(3) $y-x = 1-3 = -2$
(4) $y-x = 2-1 = 1$. This matches.
For case (ii): $K_p = 0.862$ and $K_c = 28.62$.
$\frac{K_p}{K_c} = \frac{0.862}{28.62} \approx 0.0301$.
Since $0.0301 \approx \frac{1}{32.84} = 32.84^{-1}$, we have $\Delta n_g = y - x = -1$.
Checking option (4) for (ii): $y-x = 1-2 = -1$. This also matches.
Thus, for (i) $x=1, y=2$ and for (ii) $x=2, y=1$.

subject: Chemistry
q:
A solid XY kept in an evacuated sealed container undergoes decomposition to form a mixture of gases X and Y at temperature T.

The equilibrium pressure is 10 bar in this vessel. Kp for this reaction is?
question:
A solid XY kept in an evacuated sealed container undergoes decomposition to form a mixture of gases X and Y at temperature T.

The equilibrium pressure is 10 bar in this vessel. Kp for this reaction is?
solution:
XY⇌X(S)(g)     +Y(g)

Peq=Px+Px=10

    ⇒Px=5

Kp=Px.(Py)

     =5×5=25

subject: Chemistry
q: 4.5 moles each of hydrogen and iodine is heated in a sealed ten litre vessel.At equilibrium, 3 moles of HI were found. The equilibrium constant for H2g+I2g⇌2HIg is ........
question: 4.5 moles each of hydrogen and iodine is heated in a sealed ten litre vessel.At equilibrium, 3 moles of HI were found. The equilibrium constant for H2g+I2g⇌2HIg is ........
solution:
The equilibrium moles of each reactant and product can be calculated as follows,

                                   H2g+I2g     ⇌2HIginitial                          4.5       4.5                 0At equilibrium       4.5-1.5  4.5-1.5       3

Now, using equilibrium moles, equilibrium constant calculated as shown below.
Keq=[HI]2H2I2=(3)23×3=1

subject: Chemistry
q:
For reaction: SO2( g)+12O2( g)⇌SO3( g) KP=2×1012 at 27°C and 1 atm pressure. The Kc for the same reaction is ×1013.( Nearest integer)
(Given R=0.082 L atm K-1 mol-1 )
question:
For reaction: SO2( g)+12O2( g)⇌SO3( g) KP=2×1012 at 27°C and 1 atm pressure. The Kc for the same reaction is ×1013.( Nearest integer)
(Given R=0.082 L atm K-1 mol-1 )
solution:
For reaction: SO2(g) + 12O2(g) ⇌ SO3(g)

Kp = 2×1012 at 300 K and 1 atm pressure.

The relation between equilibrium constant in terms of pressures Kp and equilibrium constant in terms of concentration Kc is

Kp = Kc × (RT)∆ng

⇒ 2×1012 = Kc × (0.082 × 300)-12

⇒ KC= 9.92 × 1012

⇒ KC = 0.992 × 1013

So, answer is 1.

subject: Chemistry
q: For the reaction $\mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g})+(1 / 2) \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})=\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}} / \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ is
question: For the reaction $\mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g})+(1 / 2) \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})=\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}} / \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}$ is
solution:
$\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}(\mathrm{RT})^{\Delta n} ; \Delta \mathrm{n}=1-\left(1+\frac{1}{2}\right)=1-\frac{3}{2}=-\frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}}=(\mathrm{RT})^{-1 / 2}$

q: A mixture of one mole of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> and 1 mole of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> is taken in a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>litre</mi></math> container and heated to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>725</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. At equilibrium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of water by mass reacts with carbon monoxide according to the equation: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mover><mo>⇌</mo><mrow/></mover><mo> </mo><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></math>. The equilibrium constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for the reaction is ________ (Nearest integer)
question: A mixture of one mole of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> and 1 mole of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> is taken in a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>litre</mi></math> container and heated to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>725</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. At equilibrium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of water by mass reacts with carbon monoxide according to the equation: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mover><mo>⇌</mo><mrow/></mover><mo> </mo><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mfenced></math>. The equilibrium constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for the reaction is ________ (Nearest integer)
solution:
CO(g)+H2O(g)⇌CO2( g)+H2( g)initial moles                           1             1moles at equilibrium          1-0.4      1-0.4    0.4            0.4Molarity                                  0.6/10       0.6/10   0.4/10   0.4/10

Now, the equilibrium constant KC=CO2H2COH2O

⇒KC=0.04×0.040.06×0.06=0.44

∴Kc×102=44

subject: Chemistry
q:
At \(-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and \(1 \mathrm{~atm}\) pressure, a cylinder is filled with equal number of \(\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{I}_2\) and \(\mathrm{HI}\) molecules for the reaction
\(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})\), the \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}\) for the process is \(x \times 10^{-1}\).
\(\mathrm{x}=\) _____
[Given : \(\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)]
question:
At \(-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) and \(1 \mathrm{~atm}\) pressure, a cylinder is filled with equal number of \(\mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{I}_2\) and \(\mathrm{HI}\) molecules for the reaction
\(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})\), the \(\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}\) for the process is \(x \times 10^{-1}\).
\(\mathrm{x}=\) _____
[Given : \(\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\)]
solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}=0 & \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{\left(\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}\right)^2}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2} \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{T}}}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{T}}}\right)^{\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}} \\ \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{HI}}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{I}_2} & \text { so } \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}=1 \\ 1=\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-1} & \mathrm{x}=10\end{array}$

subject: Chemistry
q:
2SO2( g)+O2( g)⇌2SO3( g)
In an equilibrium mixture, the partial pressures are
PSO3=43kPa;PO2=530 Pa and
PSO2=45kPa. The equilibrium constant
KP=_____×10-2. (Nearest integer)
question:
2SO2( g)+O2( g)⇌2SO3( g)
In an equilibrium mixture, the partial pressures are
PSO3=43kPa;PO2=530 Pa and
PSO2=45kPa. The equilibrium constant
KP=_____×10-2. (Nearest integer)
solution:
2SO2( g)+O2( g)=2SO3( g)
KP=pSO3( g)2pSO2g2×pO2( g)
=43×4345×45×0.530 
=172.28×10-2 kPa-1
≃172 × 10-2
Ans is 172

subject: Chemistry
q:
In aqueous solution the ionization constants for carbonic acid are $\mathrm{K}_1=4.2 \times 10^{-7}$ and $\mathrm{K}_2=4.8 \times 10^{-11}$
Select the correct statement for a saturated $0.034 \mathrm{M}$ solution of the carbonic acid.
question:
In aqueous solution the ionization constants for carbonic acid are $\mathrm{K}_1=4.2 \times 10^{-7}$ and $\mathrm{K}_2=4.8 \times 10^{-11}$
Select the correct statement for a saturated $0.034 \mathrm{M}$ solution of the carbonic acid.
options: The concentration of $\mathrm{CO}_3^{2-}$ is greater than that of $\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}$.
The concentration of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$is double that of $\mathrm{CO}_3^{2-}$.
solution:
$\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{A} \rightarrow & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-} & \mathrm{K}_1=4.2 \times 10^{-7} \\ \mathrm{~B} \rightarrow & \mathrm{HCO}_3^{-} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{CO}_3^{-2} & \mathrm{~K}_2=4.8 \times 10^{-11}\end{array}$
As $\mathrm{K}_2 \ll \mathrm{K}_1$
All major $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]_{\text {todal }} \approx\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]_{\mathrm{A}}$
and from I equilibrium, $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]_{\mathrm{A}} \approx\left[\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}\right] \approx\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]_{\text {tocal }}$
$\left[\mathrm{CO}_3^{-2}\right]$ is negligible compared to $\left[\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}\right]$or $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]_{\text {total }}$

[Given 1R=0.082 L atm K-1 mol-1

volume of vessel =2 L

NH4HS(s)⇌NH3( g)+H2S(g)

t=0  0.1 mol

t=∞0.1(1-0.2)0.1×0.20.1×0.2

Partial pressure of each gaseous component

P=nRTV=0.1×0.2×0.082×3002

=0.246 atm

⇒kP=PNH3×PH2 S=(0.246)2=0.060516

=6.05×10-2

⇒6

subject: Chemistry
q:
The number of correct statement/s involving equilibria in physical processes from the following is ________

(A) Equilibrium is possible only in a closed system at a given temperature.

(B) Both the opposing processes occur at the same rate.

(C) When equilibrium is attained at a given temperature, the value of all its parameters became equal

(D) For dissolution of solids in liquids, the solubility is constant at a given temperature.
question:
The number of correct statement/s involving equilibria in physical processes from the following is ________

(A) Equilibrium is possible only in a closed system at a given temperature.

(B) Both the opposing processes occur at the same rate.

(C) When equilibrium is attained at a given temperature, the value of all its parameters became equal

(D) For dissolution of solids in liquids, the solubility is constant at a given temperature.
solution:
The equilibrium is possible when there is no exchange of matter between system and surroundings.

At equilibrium state the forward and backward processes occur with same rate (speed)

When a system reaches equilibrium at a given temperature, the forward and reverse reactions are occurring at the same rate, and the concentrations (or partial pressures) of the reactants and products no longer change with time. At this point, the system has achieved a steady state, where the rate of the forward reaction is equal to the rate of the reverse reaction. So when equilibrium is attained, the value of all its parameters became constant.

The solubility is function of solubility product, the value of which is constant at a given temperature.

Volume $\uparrow P \downarrow$, reaction proceeds in which direction where the number of moles of gases increases.
$2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
$\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}=(2+1)-2=1$

subject: Chemistry
q:
Two solids dissociate as follows:

A s⇌B g+C g; KP1=x  atm2

D s⇌C g+E g; KP2=y  atm2

The total pressure when both the solids dissociate simultaneously is:
question:
Two solids dissociate as follows:

A s⇌B g+C g; KP1=x  atm2

D s⇌C g+E g; KP2=y  atm2

The total pressure when both the solids dissociate simultaneously is:
solution:
kp1=P1P1+P2

kp2=P2(P1+P2)

kP1+kP2=(P1+P2)2

x+y=(P1+P2)2

P1+P 2=x+y

2(P1+P2)=2x+y

PTotal=PB+PC+PE

=2(P1+P2)=2x+y
